title: POST Requests and Sending Data
description: Send data to an API using POST requests with JSON bodies.

slides.title: Beyond GET ➕
slides.content:
# Sending Data to the Server

To create or update resources, we use HTTP methods like POST, PUT, PATCH. The `fetch` API allows us to specify `method`, `headers`, and a `body`.

Common patterns:
- POST: create new resource
- PUT: replace entire resource
- PATCH: partial update
- DELETE: remove resource

For POST, we typically send JSON data.

slides.title: POST Request Example
Setting Headers
slides.content:
## Common Headers

- `Content-Type: application/json` – tells the server we're sending JSON.
- `Authorization: Bearer <token>` – for authenticated requests.
- `Accept: application/json` – we expect JSON back.

Headers are set in the `headers` object.

quizData.question: What is the correct way to send JSON data in a fetch POST request?
quizData.explanation: The `body` must be a string; use `JSON.stringify` to convert objects.

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: Always set `Content-Type` header when sending JSON, otherwise the server may not parse it correctly.
